machine.name: DailyPatientCare
machine.description: Ongoing daily patient care monitoring for an assisted living eldercare community. Recognizes normal daily routines (morning hygiene, meals, medications, evening wind-down) and detects anomalies including fall events, bathroom non-use, missed medications, and nighttime wandering.

metadata.author: Reality Engine
metadata.domain: Healthcare Operations — Assisted Living Daily Care
metadata.eventSpace: 8D binary activity space: [AMBULATE, BATHROOM, MORNING_MEAL, MEDICATION, EVENING_MEAL, ALERT, FALL, NIGHT]
metadata.outputSpace: 8D one-hot alert/completion space: [MORNING_COMPLETE, EVENING_COMPLETE, FALL_CONFIRMED, UNRESPONSIVE_FALL, BATHROOM_ALERT, MEDICATION_MISSED, WANDERING_ALERT, _]
metadata.regex: ROUTINES: (RISE)+ · BATH · BREAKFAST · MED_AM → MORNING_COMPLETE | (DINNER)+ · BATH_PM · MED_PM · SETTLE → EVENING_COMPLETE  |  ANOMALIES: (RISE)+ · FALL_EVENT · (FALL_CALL → FALL_CONFIRMED | POST_FALL_STILL → UNRESPONSIVE_FALL) | (BATH_VISIT)+ · BATH_OVERDUE → BATHROOM_ALERT | (BREAKFAST)+ · MEDICATION_MISSED_ALERT → MEDICATION_MISSED | (SETTLE)+ · NIGHT_AWAKE³ → WANDERING_ALERT

dimensions.D0_AMBULATE: Patient is up and moving — detected via bed/room exit sensor, motion tracker, or staff observation
dimensions.D1_BATHROOM: Bathroom use event — door sensor, motion sensor, or call-light acknowledgment
dimensions.D2_MORNING_MEAL: Morning meal event — dining room check-in, tray delivery scanned, or meal observation
dimensions.D3_MEDICATION: Medication administration confirmed — MAR scan, pill dispenser acknowledgment, or nurse sign-off
dimensions.D4_EVENING_MEAL: Evening meal event — dining room check-in, tray delivery, or meal observation after 4 PM
dimensions.D5_ALERT: Alert or concern flag — call-light activation, staff raised concern, sensor threshold exceeded, or time-overdue notification
dimensions.D6_FALL: Fall detection event — floor sensor, pendant alarm, accelerometer, or staff-witnessed fall
dimensions.D7_NIGHT: Nighttime context flag — set from lights-out (~9 PM) to morning rise (~6 AM); indicates nocturnal monitoring mode

vectorEncoding.semantics: GTE binary: value>=threshold → HIGH (event present/active), value<threshold → LOW (event absent). Vectors accumulate active dimensions across daily routine stages — each step adds one dimension (HD=1) to represent a completed care milestone. Anomaly vectors branch from a normal-state baseline by setting the ALERT or FALL dimension to HIGH.

namedVectors.RISE: [H,L,L,L,L,L,L,L] — patient up from bed, ambulatory only
namedVectors.MED_AM: [H,H,H,H,L,L,L,L] — ambulatory + bathroom + morning meal + AM medication
namedVectors.MED_PM: [H,H,L,H,H,L,L,L] — ambulatory + bathroom + PM medication + evening meal
namedVectors.SETTLE: [H,H,L,H,H,L,L,H] — ambulatory + bathroom + PM meds + evening meal + night flag (patient settled)
namedVectors.FALL_CALL: [H,L,L,L,L,H,H,L] — ambulatory + ALERT activated + fall (patient pressed call button)
namedVectors.POST_FALL_STILL: [L,L,L,L,L,L,H,L] — fall flag only; D0 AMBULATE=L indicates patient is not moving (unresponsive)
namedVectors.BATH_OVERDUE: [H,H,L,L,L,H,L,L] — ambulatory + bathroom context + ALERT (8+ hr non-use threshold exceeded)
namedVectors.MEDICATION_MISSED: [H,H,H,L,L,H,L,L] — morning meal context + ALERT; medication window closed without administration
namedVectors.NIGHT_AWAKE: [H,H,L,H,H,H,L,H] — settled-night baseline + ALERT flag (patient left room or is awake and moving)

singleTransitionAssumption.notes: All within-sequence transitions change exactly one binary dimension (HD=1). Morning routine: RISE→BATH(D1↑), BATH→BREAKFAST(D2↑), BREAKFAST→MED_AM(D3↑). Evening routine: DINNER→BATH_PM(D1↑), BATH_PM→MED_PM(D3↑), MED_PM→SETTLE(D7↑). Fall sequences branch from FALL_EVENT: →FALL_CALL(D5↑) or →POST_FALL_STILL(D0↓). Anomalies extend their baseline by raising D5_ALERT (HD=1). Night wandering uses identical consecutive NIGHT_AWAKE vectors (HD=0, three sequential firings of the same alert pattern).

governance.notes: Auto-backfilled by scripts/backfill-governance.mjs from primaryDomain="health-personal".  Review and tune the routing as the team owning DailyPatientCare matures.

rules.description: Event: MORNING_COMPLETE — patient successfully completed all morning care milestones
Event: EVENING_COMPLETE — patient successfully completed all evening care milestones and settled for the night
Alert: FALL_CONFIRMED — patient fell and activated call system; staff response required immediately
CRITICAL ALERT: UNRESPONSIVE_FALL — patient fell and is not moving; no call system activated; immediate staff wellness check required
Alert: BATHROOM_ALERT — patient has not used bathroom within care-plan-specified interval; assess for urinary retention, mobility difficulty, or fall risk
Alert: MEDICATION_MISSED — morning medications not administered within window; assess for refusal, availability issue, or documentation gap
Alert: WANDERING_ALERT — patient has exited room three times during nocturnal monitoring; assess for dementia-related wandering, pain, anxiety, or environmental cause

metadata.description: Patient completes all morning care milestones: rises, uses bathroom, eats breakfast, and takes AM medications.

metadata.name: Rise
metadata.description: Patient exits bed and is ambulatory. Detected via bed sensor, room motion sensor, or staff morning rounds. Repeats via Kleene-plus if patient moves around before bathroom visit.

metadata.name: Bathroom Visit
metadata.description: Patient uses bathroom as part of morning hygiene routine. Confirmed via bathroom door sensor or motion sensor. HD=1 from RISE (D1_BATHROOM: L→H).

metadata.name: Breakfast
metadata.description: Patient arrives in dining room or receives and eats breakfast tray. Confirmed via dining check-in or tray scan. HD=1 from BATH_VISIT (D2_MORNING_MEAL: L→H).

metadata.name: AM Medication Administered
metadata.description: Morning medications confirmed administered. MAR barcode scan or medication dispenser acknowledgment recorded. Completes the morning routine sequence. HD=1 from BREAKFAST (D3_MEDICATION: L→H).
Event: MORNING_COMPLETE — patient successfully completed all morning care milestones
Patient completes all evening care milestones: eats dinner, uses bathroom, takes PM medications, and settles for the night.

metadata.name: Dinner
metadata.description: Patient arrives at dining room or receives dinner tray for the evening meal. D4_EVENING_MEAL distinguishes this from breakfast (D2_MORNING_MEAL) ensuring the two routines have distinct initial vectors. Repeats via Kleene-plus across multiple dinner-hour check-ins.

metadata.name: Evening Bathroom Visit
metadata.description: Patient uses bathroom after dinner as part of evening hygiene routine. HD=1 from DINNER (D1_BATHROOM: L→H).

metadata.name: PM Medication Administered
metadata.description: Evening medications administered after dinner and bathroom visit. MAR scan confirms administration. HD=1 from BATH_PM (D3_MEDICATION: L→H).

metadata.name: Settled for Night
metadata.description: Patient returns to room, lights dim, nighttime monitoring mode activates. Bed sensor confirms patient is in bed. D7_NIGHT flag set. HD=1 from MED_PM (D7_NIGHT: L→H). This vector also serves as the initial state for nighttime wandering detection.
Event: EVENING_COMPLETE — patient successfully completed all evening care milestones and settled for the night
Patient falls during daytime ambulatory activity and activates call button or pendant alarm. Patient is alert and responsive.

metadata.name: Rise (Pre-Fall)
metadata.description: Patient is up and ambulatory. Daytime monitoring context. Repeats via Kleene-plus across normal ambulation events before the fall occurs.

metadata.name: Fall Detected
metadata.description: Fall event triggered — floor pressure sensor, wearable accelerometer, or staff witness. Patient still ambulatory (D0=H, not yet confirmed down). HD=1 from RISE (D6_FALL: L→H).

metadata.name: Call Button Activated
metadata.description: Patient or staff activates call button or pendant alarm following the fall. D5_ALERT=H indicates a response signal is in flight. Patient may be on the floor but is conscious and communicating. HD=1 from FALL_EVENT (D5_ALERT: L→H).
Alert: FALL_CONFIRMED — patient fell and activated call system; staff response required immediately
metadata.protocol: Dispatch nursing staff immediately; assess for injury; document in incident report; notify physician and family per protocol

metadata.description: Patient falls during daytime ambulatory activity and does not activate any call system. Sensor detects the fall and subsequent stillness — patient may be unconscious or incapacitated.

metadata.name: Rise (Pre-Fall)
metadata.description: Patient is up and ambulatory. Normal daytime activity before fall event. Kleene-plus allows multiple prior ambulation events.

metadata.name: Fall Detected
metadata.description: Fall event triggered by sensor. Patient still ambulatory context (D0=H). HD=1 from RISE (D6_FALL: L→H). Branches with fr-fall-event to two outcomes.

metadata.name: Post-Fall Stillness
metadata.description: Patient is on the floor and not moving. D0_AMBULATE=L (no motion detected), D6_FALL=H (fall flag persists), no ALERT signal raised. Indicates patient may be unconscious, confused, or unable to reach call button. HD=1 from FALL_EVENT (D0_AMBULATE: H→L).
CRITICAL ALERT: UNRESPONSIVE_FALL — patient fell and is not moving; no call system activated; immediate staff wellness check required
metadata.protocol: Dispatch nursing staff immediately for wellness check; assess consciousness, breathing, and injury; activate emergency response if unresponsive; do NOT attempt to move patient until assessed; notify charge nurse, physician, and family

metadata.description: Patient has not used the bathroom within the care-plan-specified window (typically 8 hours). Prior bathroom visits establish the baseline, and a time-overdue alert fires when the window lapses.

metadata.name: Bathroom Visit (Baseline)
metadata.description: Patient uses bathroom — ambulatory + bathroom sensor confirmation. Kleene-plus semantics: each successful bathroom visit resets the baseline and the overdue timer. Sequence only completes if followed by BATH_OVERDUE before the next visit.

metadata.name: Bathroom Overdue
metadata.description: Scheduled bathroom check interval exceeded with no bathroom use detected. D5_ALERT=H raised by care management system when 8-hour threshold lapses. Patient is still ambulatory (D0=H) but has not used facilities. HD=1 from BATH_VISIT (D5_ALERT: L→H).
Alert: BATHROOM_ALERT — patient has not used bathroom within care-plan-specified interval; assess for urinary retention, mobility difficulty, or fall risk
metadata.protocol: Conduct bathroom assistance offer; assess for pain, urinary retention, or toileting avoidance; document; adjust scheduled bathroom rounds if pattern persists

metadata.description: Patient has eaten breakfast but the morning medication window closes without a confirmed administration. Common causes: patient refusal, medication not available, staff oversight.

metadata.name: Breakfast (Medication Window Open)
metadata.description: Patient has eaten breakfast, establishing the medication administration window. Kleene-plus allows multiple breakfast observations (e.g., tray confirmed, meal percentage noted) before the window closes.

metadata.name: Medication Missed Alert
metadata.description: AM medication window has expired with no MAR administration confirmation. Alert raised by medication management system. D3_MEDICATION=L (not given), D5_ALERT=H (overdue). HD=1 from BREAKFAST (D5_ALERT: L→H).
Alert: MEDICATION_MISSED — morning medications not administered within window; assess for refusal, availability issue, or documentation gap
metadata.protocol: Nurse to assess patient immediately; determine if refusal (document and notify physician), medication unavailability (contact pharmacy), or administration error; administer if clinically appropriate within late-dose window; document in MAR and incident log if outside safe window

metadata.description: Patient who has settled for the night is detected leaving their room repeatedly. Three NIGHT_AWAKE events following a SETTLE baseline triggers the wandering alert — indicating a pattern rather than a single restroom visit.

metadata.name: Settled for Night (Baseline)
metadata.description: Patient is in bed with nighttime monitoring active. Same vector as the SETTLE terminal of the evening routine. This initial Kleene-plus node re-establishes the nocturnal baseline; a single restroom visit would not advance the sequence because NIGHT_AWAKE (with D5_ALERT) must fire three times consecutively.

metadata.name: Night Awake — First Exit
metadata.description: Patient detected leaving room during nocturnal monitoring window. D5_ALERT=H raised by door or motion sensor. HD=1 from SETTLE (D5_ALERT: L→H). One nighttime exit is within normal range; sequence requires two more to complete.

metadata.name: Night Awake — Second Exit
metadata.description: Second nighttime room exit detected. Same vector as the first exit (HD=0 between consecutive NIGHT_AWAKE events — sequential matching advances the sequence node pointer). Pattern is emerging; one more exit confirms wandering behavior.

metadata.name: Night Awake — Third Exit (Wandering Confirmed)
metadata.description: Third nighttime room exit in the same nocturnal monitoring period. Three repeated NIGHT_AWAKE events confirm a wandering pattern rather than isolated sleep disruption. Terminal node — fires WANDERING_ALERT output.
Alert: WANDERING_ALERT — patient has exited room three times during nocturnal monitoring; assess for dementia-related wandering, pain, anxiety, or environmental cause
metadata.protocol: Staff to check on patient immediately; ensure safety and redirect to room if wandering; assess for pain, agitation, environmental triggers (noise, light), or confusion; notify charge nurse; consider dementia care protocol if pattern is new; document and report to attending physician if recurrent

inputSequences.name: TC-01: Normal Morning Routine
inputSequences.description: ROUTINE COMPLETE: Patient follows the full morning care sequence — rises, uses bathroom, eats breakfast, and takes AM medications. Primary happy path with no friction.

metadata.scenario: 82-year-old female resident, independent with prompting. Up at 7:15 AM, bathroom at 7:20, dining room at 7:45, medication pass at 8:10. All milestones confirmed.

inputSequences.name: TC-02: Normal Morning Routine — Multiple Rises (Kleene+)
inputSequences.description: ROUTINE COMPLETE: Patient is detected up and moving three times before bathroom visit — e.g., gets up, moves to chair, gets up again. Validates Kleene-plus RISE initial semantics. Outcome unchanged.

metadata.scenario: 76-year-old male resident with early-stage Parkinson's. Takes several attempts to fully rise and move to bathroom. Motion sensor fires three times before bathroom door sensor confirms.

inputSequences.name: TC-03: Normal Evening Routine
inputSequences.description: ROUTINE COMPLETE: Patient completes all evening care milestones — attends dinner, uses bathroom, takes PM medications, and returns to room settled for the night.

metadata.scenario: 89-year-old female resident with mild dementia. Dining room at 5:30 PM, accompanied to bathroom at 6:10, medication pass at 6:25, returned to room with lights off at 8:45 PM.

inputSequences.name: TC-04: Fall — Responsive (Call Button Activated)
inputSequences.description: ANOMALY — FALL CONFIRMED: Patient falls during daytime ambulation and activates pendant alarm. Staff dispatched. Patient is alert and responsive.

metadata.scenario: 84-year-old male resident slips in hallway at 10:30 AM. Floor sensor triggers. Patient presses pendant. Nurse arrives within 3 minutes. Minor bruise to right hip. Documented per incident protocol.

inputSequences.name: TC-05: Fall — Unresponsive (No Call, Patient Still)
inputSequences.description: ANOMALY — CRITICAL UNRESPONSIVE FALL: Patient falls and does not activate any call system. Sensor detects continued stillness. Patient may be unconscious or unable to reach call device.

metadata.scenario: 91-year-old female resident with osteoporosis falls in bathroom at 2:15 PM. No call button pressed. Motion sensor shows no movement for 4 minutes. Staff found patient on floor with suspected hip fracture. Emergency services called.

inputSequences.name: TC-06: Bathroom Non-Use Alert (8+ Hour Lapse)
inputSequences.description: ANOMALY — BATHROOM ALERT: Patient had two prior bathroom visits during the day but has exceeded the care-plan bathroom interval without a new visit. Alert raised for assessment.

metadata.scenario: 79-year-old male resident with a history of UTI. Last bathroom visit confirmed at 10:00 AM. By 6:30 PM (8.5 hours), no bathroom door sensor event recorded. Care system raises alert. Staff assess for urinary retention.

inputSequences.name: TC-07: Missed Morning Medication
inputSequences.description: ANOMALY — MEDICATION MISSED: Patient ate breakfast but the AM medication administration window closed without MAR confirmation. Alert raised for immediate nursing follow-up.

metadata.scenario: 86-year-old female resident with hypertension and atrial fibrillation. Breakfast confirmed 7:50 AM. Medication pass completed for all other residents by 9:00 AM. MAR review at 9:30 AM reveals her AM medications (metoprolol, warfarin) were not documented. Nurse investigates.

inputSequences.name: TC-08: Nighttime Wandering (3 Room Exits)
inputSequences.description: ANOMALY — WANDERING ALERT: Patient settled for the night exits their room three times during nocturnal monitoring. Three exits distinguish wandering from a normal single bathroom visit.

metadata.scenario: 88-year-old male resident with moderate Alzheimer's disease. Settled at 8:30 PM. Door sensor detects exits at 11:15 PM, 12:40 AM, and 2:05 AM. Third exit triggers alert. Night staff redirects patient and notifies charge nurse. Increased monitoring scheduled.
